title: NH cold case solved 40 years after police found man’s skull in woods
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Investigators identified Warren Kuchinsky, whose skull was found in Bristol, NH, in 1986, using genetic genealogy.
The DNA Doe Project partnered with NH authorities, confirming Kuchinsky's identity through DNA testing of a family member.
No foul play is suspected in Kuchinsky's death, providing closure after he was last known alive in the mid-1970s.

title: Boston police issue scam warning for fake parking violations
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police warn of fake parking violation notices posing as official documents with a Massachusetts state seal.
The fraudulent notices include false court information and a QR code for payment, which is part of the scam.
Victims are advised to report the scam and verify parking tickets on the city's website before making any payment.

title: Mansfield man charged with illegally possessing ammunition, prosecutors say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Michael Kennedy, 48, of Mansfield, was arrested and charged with unlawfully possessing ammunition as a convicted felon.
Authorities found firearm parts, a 3D printer, and a loaded 5.7-caliber pistol in his home, prosecutors said.
Kennedy faces up to 15 years in prison and a $250,000 fine if convicted, according to federal prosecutors.

title: Here are four under the radar Red Sox players standing out in spring training
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Braiden Ward shines with 12 stolen bases in 12 games during Red Sox spring training, leading the team in steals.
Max Ferguson impresses with a grand slam, 7 RBIs, and an .821 OPS, showcasing his positional versatility.
Tayron Guerrero excels in bullpen pitching, recording six strikeouts in six innings, aiming for a spot in relief.

title: Once viewed as a rental, could Viktor Arvidsson be ideal ‘bridge’ forward for Bruins?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins retain Viktor Arvidsson at trade deadline despite interest, valuing his scoring and veteran presence over acquiring draft picks.
Arvidsson, 32, ranks second in 5-on-5 goals for Bruins and is pivotal in the team’s playoff push this season.
With $17 million cap space for 2026-27, Bruins might consider extending Arvidsson's contract for middle-line stability.

title: 5 interior offensive linemen options for Patriots after Garrett Bradbury trade
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots trade center Garrett Bradbury to Bears, prompting offensive line adjustments for the 2026 season.
Jared Wilson likely to shift from left guard to center, creating a need for a new left guard.
Potential guard options include free agents Isaac Seumalo, David Edwards, Joel Bitonio, and draft prospect Emmanuel Pregnon.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for the ‘amazing’ Make-A-Wish children
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jason Perry is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support Make-A-Wish children in Greater Massachusetts and Rhode Island.
Perry, 48, from Stockton, California, runs to honor the hope and courage of the Make-A-Wish children he has met.
For 15 years, Perry has been involved with Make-A-Wish, inspired by the resilience and joy of the children he supports.

title: Insider predicts Patriots sign Seahawks standout pass rusher, but don’t land Alec Pierce in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots expected to sign Seahawks' edge rusher Boye Mafe for three years, $48 million with $25 million guaranteed.
Boye Mafe recorded a 17.4% pass rush win rate, potentially the best for Patriots compared to last season's edge rushers.
ESPN's Dan Graziano predicts Colts' Alec Pierce signing with Chargers, not Patriots, for three years, $80 million.

title: Review & setlist: With sharp sarcasm, Jesse Welles took on war, bullies, and Trump at the House of Blues
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Jesse Welles performed at Boston's House of Blues on March 6, 2026, as part of his 'Under the Powerlines Tour'.
Welles, nominated for four Grammys in 2026, is known for his satirical and politically charged lyrics.
His setlist included original songs and covers, with themes on war, political figures, and societal issues.

title: Masataka Yoshida hits a home run in Japan’s win vs. Korea, with Drake Maye in attendance
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Masataka Yoshida hit a home run and drove in three runs in Japan's 8-6 win against Korea in the World Baseball Classic.
Japan improved to 2-0 in pool play, with Yoshida 4-for-7 overall and contributing to wins over Korea and Chinese Taipei.
Patriots quarterback Drake Maye attended the game at the Tokyo Dome, wearing a Dodgers uniform with his wife.
